findings.criterion: Gherkin steps are implemented with concrete dragstart event dispatch and event-payload observations.

evidence.quote: When the user drags the product item with data-testid "product-item-1"
const dataTransfer = new DataTransfer();\nconst dragStartEvent = new DragEvent('dragstart', { dataTransfer });\narguments[0].dispatchEvent(dragStartEvent);

findings.reasoning: Every Gherkin step has a matching step implementation. The When step dispatches a DragEvent constructed with a DataTransfer on the targeted [data-testid='product-item-1'] element, and the Then steps read dataTransfer.getData('title') and dataTransfer.getData('money') (static facts show those keys are read). Under the supplied browser data-flow rules, creating a DataTransfer, dispatching dragstart, and then reading that same DataTransfer is a valid observation of what the application's dragstart handler populated, so these implementations constitute concrete event-payload assertions of the required title and price capture.
